content:
💡 pompelmi | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Securely scanning uploaded files for malware and threats within Node.js applications without sending data to external services.
✨ Key Features: • In-memory file scanning • YARA rule integration • ZIP bomb protection • Framework adapters (Koa, Fastify, Express) • Zero cloud dependencies
📖 Summary: Pompelmi is an open-source, privacy-first file scanning solution for Node.js applications. It focuses on scanning files in memory before they are saved to disk, offering features like YARA rule integration, ZIP bomb protection, and adapters for popular web frameworks.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 onWatch | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide developers with real-time and historical insights into their AI API quota consumption across various services, helping them manage usage and costs effectively.
✨ Key Features: • Real-time AI API quota tracking for multiple providers • Lightweight background daemon (<50MB RAM)
📖 Summary: onWatch is an open-source CLI tool and background agent that monitors AI API quota usage across multiple providers like Synthetic, Z.ai, Anthropic, Codex, GitHub Copilot, and Antigravity in real time. It stores historical data locally in SQLite and provides a Material Design 3 web dashboard for easy visualization, ensuring all data remains on the user's machine with zero telemetry.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ Text-Grab | C#
🎯 Primary Use Case: Quickly and easily copying text from images, videos, or application elements on Windows that cannot otherwise be selected.
✨ Key Features: • Optical Character Recognition (OCR) for screen text • Local OCR processing using Windows API • Copy text to clipboard • Full-Screen Grab Mode (select region or click word)
📖 Summary: Text Grab is a C# Windows utility that uses Optical Character Recognition (OCR) to extract and copy text from any visible content on the screen. It leverages the Windows API for local OCR processing, offering multiple modes for flexible text capture without requiring a constant background process.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 crosspaste-desktop | Kotlin
🎯 Primary Use Case: To synchronize clipboard content across different devices (computers) on a local network or via direct connection, allowing users to copy something on one device and paste it on another without manual transfer.
✨ Key Features: • Real-time Sharing • Unified Cross-platform (Windows, macOS, Linux) • Rich Type Support (Text, Color, URL, HTML, RTF, Image, File) • End-to-End Encryption • LAN-only Serverless Architecture
📖 Summary: CrossPaste Desktop is a cross-platform application that enables seamless clipboard sharing across multiple devices. It supports various data types and ensures privacy with end-to-end encryption and a serverless, LAN-only architecture.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
